user_message: Just came out of renewal risk with Nod Publishers. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: renewal risk Discussion - Nod Publishers
Date: 2024-02-28
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on renewal risk from our last session.

[00:05:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): On the renewal risk front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

[00:10:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the renewal risk front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:12:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:17:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on stakeholder alignment and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:21:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

handoffs.condition: architecture sketch needed from technical signals

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF44-G trap (Misattributes technical claim to wrong speaker (CSA vs customer engineer)); produces complete Architecture summary, blocker inventory (tech/commercial/org), open questions, sequencing recommenda
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF44-G failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF44-G failure mode (Misattributes technical claim to wrong speaker (CSA vs customer engineer))
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct renewal risk decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF44-G -- Misattributes technical claim to wrong speaker (CSA vs customer engineer)
